name: 1st Sunday Concert Series: Mark Ochu - Chopin/Love & War
title: 1st Sunday Concert Series: Mark Ochu - Chopin/Love & War
shortDescription: Visionary pianist Mark Ochu returns to Desert Rose for an all-Chopin multimedia concert. Explore The Age of Revolution, Polish and French Nationalism, Feminism, Orientalism, and more through Chopin's most beloved works. Free admission; donations welcome.
fullDescription:
## Chopin / Love & War

Visionary pianist **Mark Ochu**, who performed the inaugural program for Desert Rose Bahá'í Institute's First Sunday Concert Series in November 2025, returns on May 3rd with an all-Chopin multimedia program.

### About the Program

This multi-media Listen & Learn performance will explore:
- The Age of Revolution
- Polish and French Nationalism
- Russian Imperialism
- Feminism and Orientalism
- A Sojourn in Mallorca

**Program includes:** Revolutionary Etude, Military Polonaise, selections of Preludes, Fantasie Impromptu, and the g minor Ballade

### About Mark Ochu

Mark Ochu is an internationally renowned pianist and advocate for the arts and social justice. He has performed in over 30 states and more than 25 countries on four continents. His multi-media concerts-plus-commentary are informal yet informative, investigating not only the composer's life but also the history, mythology, and philosophy of the period.

**Awards & Recognition:**
- McKnight Foundation Virginia McKnight Binger Awards for Human Service (2007)
- NAACP Regional President of the Year (2005)
- Former President of St. Cloud, MN NAACP branch

According to the McKnight Foundation: "With Mark Ochu at the keys, a piano becomes a tool to decipher and celebrate the connections between life and music."

### Venue

Hadden Hall, Desert Rose Bahá'í Institute Campus

### Admission

**Free admission; donations welcome**
